mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the consumer price index over three months signals higher inflation expectations, which compels investors to demand a larger term premium on long-term bonds, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward regression shows that a 1% MoM increase in CPIAUCSL does not correspond to a positive median yield change over the next 20 trading days in at least 60% of non-overlapping episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the unemployment rate below 4.5% triggers market repricing of labor tightness, as it indicates the economy is near full capacity and wage-driven inflation will pressure the Federal Reserve to raise short-term rates, causing the 10-year yield to rise over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In all instances where UNRATE falls to 4.5% or below, the 20-day forward change in the 10-year yield is negative or zero in more than half of the cases, or the average yield change is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production rises and the unemployment rate simultaneously falls, the economy sends a synchronized signal of robust aggregate demand. This elevates expectations of future credit demand and inflation, raising the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In rolling 12-month windows, the joint signal from a positive INDPRO change and negative UNRATE change fails to predict positive yield changes at least 55% of the time over the next 20 days.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a sequence of federal funds rate increases raises the expected path of short-term rates; through the term structure expectations hypothesis, this pushes the 10-year yield up over a 60-trading-day horizon as the market prices in further tightening.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 25bp over a 3-month period
failure_condition: Over any tightening cycle identified by a rising FEDFUNDS, the 60-day forward yield change is negative or zero in more than 40% of the triggered periods, or the average change is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in retail sales (above 1.5% month-over-month) signals unexpectedly strong consumer spending, which lifts growth expectations and increases demand for credit, driving the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When RSAFS MoM exceeds 1.5%, the subsequent 20-day yield change is negative in more than 50% of occurrences, or the mean yield change is indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth, measured month over month, provides a timely read on real economic supply capacity. A rising trend in output signals stronger economic momentum that increases the demand for capital, causing the 10-year yield to rise over a short horizon of 5 trading days as markets react to the data release.
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO MoM change and subsequent 5-day yield change is not positive and significantly different from zero at the 5% level over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls increase strongly and the unemployment rate simultaneously falls, the labor market signals tightening that feeds into wage inflation expectations. This joint signal amplifies the impact on long-term yields, pushing the 10-year yield up over the next 20 trading days more than either series alone.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion, any regime with low unemployment
failure_condition: The interaction term (PAYEMS change * UNRATE change) in a linear regression of 20-day yield changes is not statistically significant (t-stat < 2) over any 5-year rolling window, or the model with interaction does not improve out-of-sample R^2 over the additive model.

mechanism_narrative: In a stagflationary regime where industrial production contracts while consumer prices accelerate, the market fears persistent inflation despite weak real growth. This degrades the real return on bonds and pushes the 10-year yield upward over a 20-trading-day horizon as investors demand higher nominal compensation.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition holds, the subsequent 20-day yield change is negative or zero in more than 50% of cases, or the average yield change is not positive and statistically significant (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).
